title: symphytum asperum var. armeniacum
intro: Symphytum asperum var. armeniacum, also known as Armenian comfrey or rough comfrey, is a perennial herb in the borage family with a long history of traditional use. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which make it useful for wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and supporting bone health. The health benefits of Armenian comfrey include its ability to accelerate tissue repair, reduce swelling and pain, and promote bone growth, making it a popular remedy for sprains, strains, and broken bones. Armenian comfrey can be prepared as a herbal tea, salve, or infusion, and is also used topically as a poultice or compress to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
